rationale: Deng et al. (2016) is not merely listed as generic background; it is treated as a specific prior study on shelf orientation within the citing paper's conceptual positioning and comparison table. The citing paper contrasts its own co-location/categorization focus against Deng et al.'s orientation focus, includes orientation in Lab Study 2, and interprets null orientation findings via Deng et al.'s single-item-choice boundary condition. The citation also informs measurement practice (choice satisfaction items), indicating a meaningful functional role in framing and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Deng et al. (2016) as an important adjacent anchor on orientation-based shelf effects to position its distinct contribution on categorization congruency and co-location. It also leverages Deng et al. for design/measurement choices and for interpreting orientation-related results.
